svg.font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
svg.text: Anatomy of a cuga-app — bird_invocable_api_creator (★ standout)
Closed-loop synthesis: turn a Bird-SQL question into a validated invocable tool, anchored against gold SQL on real sqlite.

g.text: # Per-question thread_id keeps synthesis state isolated across batches.

svg.text: REQUEST FLOW — POST /question/california_schools/28 (qid 28: "schools whose enrollment difference exceeds the avg for locally-funded schools")

g.text: PHASE A · ORIENT — read the schema, sample real values, capture the oracle answer
execute gold SQL on real DB
— this is the target the agent must hit
PHASE B · DECOMPOSE — reason about the gold SQL's shape; pick the right number of tools
Gold SQL has subquery in WHERE → strong decomposition signal → 2 reusable tools.
Slot examples must NOT include 'Locally funded' (the answer); pick from other DISTINCT values.
PHASE C · SYNTHESIZE — register tools, smoke-test against real sqlite, record the sequence
PHASE D · VALIDATE — re-run the recorded sequence and compare set-equal to the gold result
Why this matters — what the loop delivers that a one-shot LLM script cannot
Every emitted tool has run vs. gold. Trustworthy by construction.
Forbidden names + SQL-skeleton dup + Jaccard ≥0.85 → reject.
Tool registered for q28 visible to q47 → reuse, smaller API surface.
Output is a runnable MCP server
dataset_emit → <db>_mcp_server.py — point any agent at it.
